message:
fix(io,net,reactor): shutdown propagation, TCP close semantics, epoll deregister atomicity

Three independent networking/IO correctness fixes:

1. io/copy: CopyBidirectional now calls poll_shutdown() on the writer
   side after the reader EOF is received and the buffer is fully
   flushed. Previously the transfer was considered "done" without
   signaling the writer, meaning protocols that rely on graceful
   half-close (TLS close_notify, HTTP/2 GOAWAY) would never receive
   the shutdown signal. Adds shutdown_done flag to TransferState and
   applies symmetrically to both a->b and b->a directions. Test
   verifies both sides receive shutdown.

2. net/tcp/virtual_tcp: VirtualTcpListener close semantics tightened:
   - close() now clears the pending connections queue, preventing
     accept() from returning stale connections after close
   - inject_connection() and VirtualConnectionInjector::inject() both
     return early if the listener is already closed
   - accept() and poll_accept() check closed BEFORE checking the
     connections queue (was reversed), preventing a race where a
     closed listener could still yield a queued connection
   Two new tests: close_drops_pending_connections and
   inject_after_close_is_ignored.

3. reactor/epoll: EpollReactor::deregister() now holds the
   registrations lock across the entire operation and only removes
   the token from the map after the kernel epoll_ctl(DEL) succeeds
   or the error is confirmed benign. Uses fcntl(F_GETFD) to
   distinguish "target fd closed" EBADF (benign, kernel already
   cleaned up) from "reactor epoll fd invalid" EBADF (serious).
   Explicitly drops the lock in each match arm for clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
